title: RuleSentry v3 Policy
description: Versioned policy that groups rule references under an optional jurisdictional lens. Supports both legacy rule-based and new category-based policy definitions. v3.1 renames 'category_includes' to 'rule_categories' for clarity, adds policy inheritance via 'extends', and adds rule override system.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for this document (optional but recommended).

type.description: Top-level entity type discriminator. Use 'policy' for all new policies. 'pack' is DEPRECATED since v3.1 — use 'policy' with rule_categories for multi-category compliance bundles.
type.$comment: DEPRECATED: 'pack' will be removed in the next major version.

description.description: Detailed description of what this policy does.

priority.description: Optional priority for this policy when multiple policies are active. Higher numbers = higher priority = evaluated first in multi-policy scenarios. If two policies produce different decisions, the most restrictive wins (block > transform > allow). Policies with higher priority can short-circuit evaluation by returning 'block'. Default: 50 if omitted.

extends.description: Optional policy ID to inherit from. Child policy inherits all rule_categories, jurisdiction settings, and configuration from parent. Child can add additional categories and override specific rules. Supports single-level inheritance only (child cannot extend a policy that also extends another). Use overrides array to customize inherited rules.

overrides.description: Optional rule-level overrides to customize behavior without duplicating rule definitions. Applied after policy expansion. Supports wildcard matching (e.g., 'financial.*' matches all financial category rules). Use this to adjust severity, add allowlists, change effects, or boost priority for specific rules.

items.description: Override configuration for one or more rules (supports wildcards).

rule_id.description: Rule ID to override. Supports wildcards: 'financial.*' overrides all financial rules, 'government.ssn_*' overrides all SSN variants. Use exact ID for single rule.

severity.description: Override severity level for conflict resolution and alerting.

allowlist.description: Values to allow (bypass detection). Merged with rule's existing allowlist. Use for test data, placeholder values, or known safe patterns.

effect.description: Override the rule's effect (decision and transforms). Completely replaces rule's original effect.

priority.description: Override evaluation priority. Higher = evaluated first. Useful for ensuring specific rules run before others within the same policy.

enabled.description: Override enabled state. Set false to disable rule without removing it from policy. Default: true.

reason.description: Optional explanation for why this override exists. Useful for audit trail and documentation.
examples: Upgraded to CRITICAL for healthcare compliance
Allowlist includes test SSNs from QA environment

profiles.description: Cross-cutting profile identifiers that this policy implements or enforces. Profiles enable grouping policies by operational context (e.g., compliance framework, environment, deployment stage). A policy inherits all profiles from its constituent rules, but can also declare additional policy-level profiles. Examples: ['hipaa', 'production'], ['pci-dss', 'staging'], ['gdpr', 'multi_tenant'].

regions.description: Geographic regions this policy targets. Declares the intended jurisdictional scope of the policy (e.g., GDPR pack targets EU, US Compliance targets US). Used to: (1) validate compatibility with user's selected region, (2) enable smart UI filtering (show/hide packs based on relevance), (3) auto-filter included rules by region intersection. Defaults to GLOBAL if omitted.

rule_categories.description: Category-based rule inclusion (v3.1 renamed from 'category_includes' for clarity). Policies reference entire data categories (government, financial, healthcare, etc.) instead of individual rules. Categories are groups of rules that detect the same type of data.

items.description: A data category to include in this policy with optional filtering.

category_id.description: ID of the data category to include (e.g., 'government', 'healthcare', 'financial'). Refers to rule category_id field.

mode.description: Include mode: 'all' includes all rules from category, 'selective' only includes specified rules.

include_rules.description: When mode='selective', list of specific rule IDs to include from this category.

exclude_rules.description: Optional list of rule IDs to exclude from this category.

optional_rules.description: Rule IDs that are optional (user can toggle on/off).

default_enabled_rules.description: For optional rules, which ones are enabled by default.

rules.description: Direct rule references for explicit rule inclusion. Can be used alongside rule_categories and rule_filters.

items.description: Reference to a specific version of a rule.

pinned_version.description: Specific rule version to use (pinned; no implicit upgrades).

priority.description: Optional priority for this rule within the policy. Higher numbers = higher priority = evaluated first. Used for evaluation order and conflict resolution within the policy. If omitted, rules are evaluated in array order. Range: 0-1000, default: 50.

attached_at.description: When this rule was attached to this policy (ISO 8601).

attached_by.description: Who attached this rule to this policy.

notes.description: Optional notes about why this rule is included.

rule_filters.description: Dynamic rule selection filters. Rules matching any filter are included in the policy. Evaluated at runtime for flexible, attribute-based rule composition.

items.description: Filter criteria for dynamic rule selection. Multiple properties within a filter are combined with AND logic.

by_region.description: Include rules that apply to any of these regions.

by_profile.description: Include rules tagged with any of these profiles.

by_category.description: Include rules from any of these categories.

by_tags.description: Include rules tagged with any of these tags.

by_severity.description: Include rules with any of these severity levels.

metadata.description: Additional policy metadata (e.g., beta status, frameworks).

anyOf.description: Dynamic rule selection by attributes (region, profile, tags, severity)
